title: BlackBerry's Quiet Comeback: QNX Software Powers 275 Million Vehicles
text: BlackBerry Ltd. has transformed its business focus from smartphones to enterprise software, with its QNX technology now embedded in 275 million vehicles worldwide. This shift has positioned the company for renewed relevance in the automotive and IoT sectors. The company achieved its first profitable streak in a decade, posting four consecutive profitable quarters. Fourth-quarter results showed revenue of $141.7 million, exceeding guidance, with QNX contributing $65.8 million in revenue and Secure Communications adding $67.3 million. QNX President John Wall noted that the software operates invisibly in vehicles, powering safety-critical systems across automotive, medical, industrial, rail, and robotics markets. The company's royalty backlog reached approximately $865 million, suggesting substantial future revenue potential. BlackBerry recently expanded its collaboration with Nvidia, combining QNX OS for Safety 8.0 with Nvidia IGX Thor to create platforms for real-time AI control in regulated environments. For fiscal 2026, the company projects revenue between $504-$534 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $69-$84 million.
